evidence_quotes.quote: Video games should also be protected against destruction.
Nowadays, if a game is no longer supported by servers, the game will not be played for those who have previously spent money or received digital content.

paragraphs.text: Video games should also be protected against destruction. Nowadays, if a game is no longer supported by servers, the game will not be played for those who have previously spent money or received digital content. As the game is bought by people and not rented, it is totally unacceptable that a company makes it impossible to use the product I buy. The solution is that an end of life plan should be drawn up at the design stage of the toy on how to operate the game independently of the publisher for fans (if online games/self hosting/private servers) or offline if it is mainly a single player. It is unacceptable for it to become unusable (such as Ubisoft’s toy as Crew) after the toy has ceased to be subsidised. There are quite a lot of current toys at risk, but it would be good for toys to be prepared with this awareness in the future (end of life plan). The EU petition Stop Destroying Videogames has already been signed by over 1.4 million people. It would finally be worthwhile to take this into account. Software, audio and visual products are the same goods as physical goods. If humans take something after that person’s belt and there is nothing to prevent the manufacturer from using it at any time later, but this is currently the case. This needs to change as a matter of urgency!
